title: Split config: domain.Workspace (synced) + domain.Local (gitignored) + adapter/config loaders
acceptance_criteria: Workspace struct (committed at .tasks/workspace.json): schema_version, workspace_name, default_project, claim_ttl_minutes, heartbeat_interval_minutes, critical_path, git.{auto_commit, auto_sync_seconds}. Stable across VMs; safe to git sync.

Local struct (gitignored at .tasks/local.json): default_agent, agent_role, agent_capabilities. Per-VM, never synced. This split resolves SQ-003 — see docs/spec-questions.md — and intentionally diverges from spec §5.1's single config.json design to avoid the multi-VM merge-conflict trap.
domain.WorkspaceLoader port (Load/Save) and domain.LocalLoader port (Load/Save) are separate so usecases can request only what they need.

Env-var overrides per §7.1 / §18: TASK_AGENT_ID overrides Local.default_agent; TASK_AGENT_ROLE overrides Local.agent_role; TASK_AGENT_CAPABILITIES (CSV) overrides Local.agent_capabilities. Resolved in adapter/config.LoadLocalEffective.

Loading a missing local.json returns sensible defaults (agent_role=human, default_agent=$USER or empty, empty capabilities). Loading a missing workspace.json after task init has run is an integrity error.
Schema validation rejects unknown fields in either file with ErrSchemaInvalid.
Tests cover: load/save round-trip for each file, missing-file defaults, invalid-schema rejection, env-var override precedence (env > local.json > built-in default).
